item.title: The Great Migration Continues
item.description: Americans are still fleeing expensive governments.

item.title: Trump Needs a More Ambitious Ballroom
item.description: The new White House addition should speak to the nation’s past and its aspirations for the future.

item.title: Strong Farms Start With Legal Foreign Labor
item.description: Thousands of positions go unfilled, which means apples go unpicked and tomatoes rot in fields.

item.title: The WTO Isn’t Dead, but America Is Breaking It
item.description: Reform can’t happen if the U.S. keeps pretending it didn’t help cripple the institution it’s now eulogizing.

item.title: Vance’s Risky Hungary Election Power Play
item.description: Orbán’s domestic standing has weakened significantly.

item.title: Pepper…and Salt
In Gut We Trust?
item.description: I think Trump shocked his followers with his vulgar, threatening social-media posts about the Iran war.

item.title: The Truth About Medicare Advantage
item.description: About half of seniors use a program that is under Democratic assault.

item.title: New York Times Keeping the Climate Faith
item.description: Biden ‘embraced the science.’

item.title: Sonia Sotomayor Profiles Brett Kavanaugh
item.description: The Justice says her colleague ‘probably doesn’t really know any person who works by the hour.’

item.title: U.S. Fiscal Reality: Medicaid Rises 10%, Defense Only 4%
item.description: How about using reconciliation 2.0 for more Medicaid reforms?

item.title: Religion Belongs in American History Class
item.description: Teachers tend to avoid the topic of faith. A new Utah law aims to remedy that problem.

item.title: Trump’s Perilous Straits
item.description: Americans hate losing wars, and he faces political trouble if he bugs out of Iran.

item.title: New England Considers the Nuclear Option
item.description: The region’s governors acknowledge the limits of ‘renewable’ energy.

item.title: A Good Husband Walks
item.description: My wife complained that I tended to stride ahead of her—so I changed.

item.title: Can Any War Live Up to the Idea of Justice?
item.description: The goal of the U.S. in Iran isn’t revenge, which some pacifists and liberals insist it is.

item.title: Maine Shouldn’t Miss the Infrastructure Boom
item.description: A moratorium on data centers would send the wrong signal at the wrong time.

item.title: New Data Centers Won’t Be Draining Us Dry
item.description: The U.S. loses over two trillion gallons of water annually to leaky pipes, a far larger problem.

item.title: How Trump Miscalculated in Iran
item.description: He followed his Venezuela model, not accounting for the ideological character of the regime in Tehran.

item.title: Budapest Isn’t a Shining MAGA City on a Hill
item.description: Hungarians turn out to be pretty European, and Orbán is more opportunist than he is revolutionary.

item.title: Pepper…and Salt
Digital Assets Rules Need Clarity
item.description: U.S. financial regulation sets the standard for the world. Congress needs to act to keep it that way.

item.title: Trump Declares Premature Victory in Iran
item.description: Tehran is still a threat to the Strait and may retain enriched uranium.

item.title: Wisconsin Swings to the Left
item.description: The GOP loses another court seat and the Waukesha mayor’s race.

item.title: Banks, Regulators and ‘Reputational Risk’
item.description: A new Trump rule would bar debanking for political reasons.

item.title: What Victory Looks Like in Iran
item.description: Trump can’t negotiate away free movement in the Strait of Hormuz or highly enriched uranium.

item.title: Does Iran Have Enough Experts to Analyze Trump?
item.description: Depleted supplies could make negotiations difficult.

item.title: Where the GOP’s Future Is on the Ballot
item.description: Four upcoming races will be early signals of how the 2028 free-for-all could play out.

item.title: A Place Like Greenland, Only Unclaimed
item.description: Trump should assert American sovereignty over Antarctica’s Marie Byrd Land.

item.title: The U.S.-Iran War Isn’t Over
item.description: Washington advanced its aims more than Tehran did, and a cease-fire isn’t a permanent settlement.

item.title: Pakistan Has Put Itself Back on the Diplomatic Map
item.description: Its army chief consolidates his power at home by brokering a cease-fire between Iran and the U.S.

item.title: Forgotten Heroes of Vietnam’s Rescue Missions
item.description: Many of those pilots are still alive.

item.title: Tyndale’s Translation and More’s Resistance
item.description: More helped to cement the church’s opposition to Protestantism in the early years of the Reformation.

item.title: How Trump Can Make the World a Safer Place
item.description: If Iran’s nuclear threat is successfully dismantled, it will have been because of the president’s courageous actions.

item.title: Pepper…and Salt
Is Antisemitism on the Rise Among College Students?
item.description: Students discuss the relationship young people have with Jews and the Jewish faith.

item.title: The ‘Judge of Death’ and the Iranian Resistance
item.description: Dissidents need guns now.

item.title: JD Vance Stars in Our Man in Budapest
item.description: The Veep meddles in a Hungarian election to help himself in 2028.

item.title: The U.N. Offers No Help on Hormuz
item.description: Russia and China force reluctant U.S. allies to decide for themselves.

item.title: The Democratic Race to the Left
item.description: In Maine’s Senate primary, Janet Mills chases Graham Platner on a data-center ban and a huge tax increase.

item.title: Robert Mueller and Trump II
item.description: If Democrats take power in Congress again, let them study their mistakes.

item.title: Another Fish Story From the WTO
item.description: The organization has failed, and the U.S. will chart its own course on trade policy.

item.title: The American Middle Class Keeps Getting Richer
item.description: Study after study finds that the populists of left and right are wrong in their predictions of doom.

item.title: High Gasoline Prices Signal Trouble to Older Americans
item.description: If you lived through the 1970s oil shocks, you may overreact when you see $4 a gallon at the pump.

item.title: In Estonia, NATO Practices for a Drone War
item.description: The multinational battle group showed progress, but also raised questions about the allies’ readiness.

item.title: Trump Can Make America Optimistic Again
item.description: Put aside grievances and keep reminding us why the U.S. is the envy of the world.

item.title: Blitz Iran’s Water Infrastructure, Lose the War
item.description: It would hand the country a propaganda victory and create a humanitarian catastrophe of epic proportions.

item.title: Air Canada Chooses Blunder Over ‘Bonjour’
item.description: Nobody forced Mr. Rousseau to make a video and demonstrate his linguistic shortcomings.

item.title: The Chokepoint Holding My Country Hostage
item.description: Mauritius imports every liter of fuel it consumes.

item.title: Pepper…and Salt
Trump’s Ultimatum Target List in Iran
item.description: An escalation shouldn’t punish the people more than the regime.

item.title: The Farm Labor Shortfall Bites
item.description: U.S. workers applied for only 182 of 415,000 jobs advertised last year.

item.title: Jamie Dimon Warns on Private Credit
item.description: The JPMorgan CEO puts the mini-panic in financial markets in useful perspective.

item.title: The Constitution Could Let Noncitizens Vote
item.description: States could loosen their rules, and an amendment is the only way to prevent it.

item.title: Pope Leo XIV Goes to War
item.description: The pontiff wrongly suggests that pacifism is the sole acceptable moral position.

item.title: The Iran War Around the World
item.description: China sees opportunity. Europe is divided. Aside from Tehran itself, Russia has the most to lose.

item.title: NATO Allies Are Quietly Helping the U.S. in Iran
item.description: Spain is the exception, but the U.K., Italy, Germany, Portugal and even France are doing their parts.

item.title: Blue States Are Insulating Unions From Debate
item.description: Democratic-run states are advancing a one-sided agenda that favors public unions at the expense of workers.

item.title: Bernie Sanders Is Wrong About AI Innovation
item.description: Artificial intelligence combined with human ingenuity will unlock new doors.

item.title: Britannia Doesn’t Rule the Waves Anymore
item.description: The once-great British Royal Navy is now a shell of its former self.

item.title: Back-to-Basics Governance
item.description: Democrats need a platform focused on stability and substance over stunts.

item.title: Why ‘No Kings’ Protesters Won’t Win the Long Game
item.description: Successful social movements throughout history have articulated a clear message and concrete goals.

item.title: Is Anyone Still Calling Her a Moderate?
item.description: After a big 2025 win, Gov. Spanberger is barely above water in new Virginia poll.

item.title: Assisted Suicide Is a Threat to Freedom
item.description: Noelia Castillo Ramos was in despair. By helping her kill herself, the Spanish state destroyed her autonomy.

item.title: A Judge Mistakes the Claude Chatbot for a Person
item.description: Jed Rakoff holds that a defendant waived attorney-client confidentiality by asking AI about his case.

item.title: Pepper…and Salt
Elon Musk’s Starship Heavy Could Revolutionize Warfare
item.description: The U.S. could keep powerful munitions in orbit to be deployed quickly and without risk to American troops.

item.title: Trump Wants to Liberate 401(k)s
item.description: Union pensions invest in private equity. Why not let others do it too?

item.title: A Daring Rescue Inside Iran
item.description: The mission is more evidence of U.S. military competence and bravery.

item.title: New York’s Sneak Tax Attack
item.description: As the SALT exemption grows, Albany wants to reduce its work-around for pass-throughs.

item.title: I Give Up on These Defeatists
item.description: From ‘No Kings’ and Iran to data centers, too many Americans are fighting progress.

item.title: Mexico’s Missing and the Morena Party
item.description: Sheinbaum’s initial steps to restore public confidence in the government fall short.

item.title: Europe Looks Away From Troubling Signs in Ukraine
item.description: The Continent’s leaders, seeking a buffer against Russia, ignore perils to democracy in Kyiv.

item.title: The Cartoonist vs. the General
item.description: Eisenhower overruled Patton. Bill Mauldin kept on drawing.

item.title: Harmeet Dhillon Is No Civil-Rights Enforcer
item.description: Under her leadership, the Justice Department’s Civil Rights Division fails to protect the rights of the most vulnerable members of our society.

item.title: Cable’s Critical Role in Wireless Competition
item.description: SpaceX’s Starlink gets too much credit as a driver of customer savings. Cable-provided plans last year saved consumers $5 billion.

item.title: Provocative Podcast Pundits Degrade National Dialogue
item.description: The digital swamp is made worse by influencers who refuse to engage in thoughtful, fact-based debate.

item.title: Will GLP-1 Drugs Transform the Food Business?
item.description: Probably not. People are likely to eat smaller and fewer meals, but not necessarily healthier ones.

item.title: Voting in California: All in Favor, Say ‘Arf’
item.description: An Orange County dog was allowed to cast a ballot, and Democrats try to stop a voter-ID initiative.

item.title: Pepper…and Salt
The North Korea Lesson for Iran
item.description: Diplomacy failed to stop Pyongyang from getting the bomb. Trump didn’t make the same mistake.

item.title: The ObamaCare Crisis That Isn’t
item.description: The large coverage losses and premium hikes haven’t happened.

item.title: Paring the Government Payroll
item.description: Federal jobs have fallen by nearly 12% since the start of Trump II.

item.title: The Russian People Want the Internet Back
item.description: Online freedom becomes a burning issue as the government shuts down popular discontent.

item.title: Trump and Bismarck in the Same Sentence
item.description: With his Iran war, the U.S. president is creating chaos for the world and wider options for himself.

item.title: About that Bondi-Trump Lawfare
item.description: Sometimes the president’s opponents really do deserve scrutiny.

item.title: Even Southern States Should Worry About Migration to Florida
item.description: School choice attracted the Quigley family, who were otherwise inclined to move to Georgia.

item.title: The Democrats’ ObamaCare Quagmire
item.description: Their solution to the law’s disastrous effects is simply more of the same.

item.title: The ‘AI Detector’ as Defamation Machine
item.description: Pangram said three of my writers produced ‘AI-generated’ articles. That didn’t hold up. Was Mia Ballard railroaded too?

item.title: Birthright Citizenship and the Constitution
item.description: Readers debate whether the 14th Amendment confers U.S. citizenship on babies whose parents are in the U.S. illegally or temporarily.

item.title: Speed Bumps on the Road to Self-Driving Cars
item.description: The software is ready for prime time, so what’s holding back the autonomous vehicle industry? Infrastructure and permitting barriers.

item.title: The Blue State Exodus Enriches Red States
item.description: Between 2012 and 2023, high-tax states lost a massive amount of taxable income to low-tax states.

item.title: Pepper…and Salt
A Republic, Not a Mood
item.description: The presidency has become too dependent on the ego and temperament of the man in office.

item.title: At the Supreme Court, Trump Courts Defeat
item.description: Why would he show up and antagonize the justices? He has an able team of lawyers.

item.title: Cuba Waits in Darkness for Easter
item.description: The communists can’t suppress the yearning for something greater than the state.

item.title: Trump May Turn Your 401(k) Into an Old-Fashioned Pension
item.description: The Labor Department’s proposed rule is a game changer for savings—and draws predictable fire from the left.

item.title: Why Britain Can’t Afford a Navy
item.description: With military readiness in doubt, a ‘reformer’ promises more welfare.

item.title: Pam Bondi Gets Beached as Attorney General
item.description: Trump needs more than a lawfare yes-woman at the Justice Department.

item.title: A German Official Talks Like Jimmy Carter—and That’s Good
item.description: The center-left finance minister offers reforms that have real promise for spurring economic growth.

item.title: AI Is a Threat to Everything the American People Hold Dear
item.description: It kills jobs, equality, connection, democracy and maybe the human race. Congress must act.

item.title: Trump’s Budget Breakthrough for Defense
item.description: He’s following through on his vow to spend $1.5 trillion in fiscal 2027.

item.title: I’m Gay, but That Doesn’t Make Me ‘Queer’
item.description: My sexuality doesn’t require me to reject all that is ‘normal’ and ‘legitimate.’

item.title: Defeatists on the Left, Pollyannas on the Right
item.description: From Pickaxe Mountain to the Strait of Hormuz, so much that matters still hangs in the balance.

item.title: Job Market Cools at Small Firms
item.description: The latest NFIB survey finds fewer firms planning to raise pay.

item.title: When Will the Election-Fraud Debate End?
item.description: The Senate is pushing a solution in search of a problem.

item.title: Getting America’s Wireless Future Done Right
item.description: The FCC has the ability to make EchoStar honor its obligations as a condition of transaction approval.

item.title: Trump Must Hit Iran’s Infrastructure to Win
item.description: Restraint has its place, but so does the recognition that failing to act decisively can extend the suffering we seek to end.

item.title: Pepper…and Salt
Trump Says He Will Finish the Job in Iran
item.description: The President made his best case so far for attacking the regime in Tehran.

item.title: For Civil Liberties’ Sake, Reauthorize Section 702
item.description: It may be America’s most valuable tool for foreign-intelligence collection—and letting it lapse would destroy its safeguards.

item.title: NASA Heads Back to the Moon
item.description: The U.S. aims high with Artemis II, as China seeks to land by 2030.

item.title: The NFL Tests Its Antitrust Exemption
item.description: The league is now the goliath, as it seeks to squeeze its weaker network partners.

item.title: Democrats vs. the Freedom Foundation
item.description: New York and Hawaii are copying a toxic union-protection law.

item.title: Bomb Iran but Blow Up NATO?
item.description: It would be the height of folly and help Moscow, Tehran and Beijing.

item.title: The 2026 Races to Watch for 2028 Results
item.description: Key primaries will guide Democratic presidential hopefuls to the left or center.

item.title: A Canadian CEO Is Lost in Translation
item.description: A plane crash leads to an unseemly fight over bilingualism.

item.title: ‘Liberation Day,’ One Year Later
item.description: Trump’s tariffs didn’t spur economic growth but did encourage trade between spurned U.S. partners.

item.title: ‘Life After California’
item.description: The Los Angeles Times reports on thriving former residents of the Golden State.

item.title: The Courts Should Treat Social Media Like Cigarettes
item.description: These apps knowingly promote harmful addiction in minors. Tech companies should be held accountable.

item.title: Why the International Criminal Court Isn’t Taking On Iran
item.description: The ICC’s silence reveals its politicization and the urgent need for tougher American sanctions.

item.title: Civil Resistance and the Creation of America
item.description: In politics it is particularly important to demonstrate publicly when the electorate feels their representatives aren’t listening to them.

item.title: The Chicago Bears Read Their Tax Bill First
item.description: Here’s how to understand the team’s motivations.

item.title: Bad Policy Made Housing Unaffordable
item.description: We are simply asking the Trump administration to reform an outdated system.

item.title: Pepper…and Salt
How to Serve Your Country if You Aren’t in the Military
item.description: Students discuss the different ways Americans can contribute to their nation.

item.title: Birthright Citizenship Hits the Supreme Court
item.description: Trump’s order to exclude the children of illegal immigrants is legal revisionism.

item.title: China Keeps Trying to Punish Tokyo
item.description: Trump can help an ally by stopping in Tokyo on his way to Beijing.

item.title: Trump and the Hormuz Stakes
item.description: The U.S. won’t escape damage if the Strait remains largely closed.

item.title: Hormuz, Shmormuz
item.description: The U.S. needs to define its goals and let other problems take care of themselves.

item.title: The Iran War Doesn’t Have to Be a Rerun of ‘That ’70s Show’
item.description: Rising energy prices turn into recessions only if bad government policies compound their effects.

item.title: The Tragic Tale of Tiger Woods
item.description: Sports heroes face inner demons as the rest of us do but sometimes have to deal with them publicly.

item.title: Life Looks Different From the Stars
item.description: The first moon trip in decades raises the big questions of existence.

item.title: Gallup: Americans Less Worried
item.description: Is there something about a Trump White House that makes Americans exhale?

item.title: Trump Is Right on Birthright Citizenship
item.description: The 14th Amendment’s authors would exclude illegal and visiting aliens from U.S. ‘jurisdiction.’

item.title: America Faces Tough Choices in Iran
item.description: Even taking Kharg Island will require significant forces and a wider air operation.

item.title: St. Thomas More and the Reckoning of History
item.description: It’s time to overcome the distorted narratives of the sixteenth century and work toward greater Christian unity.

item.title: The Pilot Who Sent Goddard to Guggenheim
item.description: It’s worthwhile to recall that aviation pioneer Charles Lindbergh brought about that connection.

item.title: TSA Lines Stretch While Congress Flies Free
item.description: How can we keep leaders who don’t suffer the consequences of their poor governance?

item.title: Pepper…and Salt
California’s Attorney General Faces Texas Justice
item.description: Exxon Mobil’s defamation case against Rob Bonta looks likelier to go to trial.

item.title: Beasley Allen Gets the Legal Boot
item.description: A judge bounces the notorious plaintiffs firm from the J&J trial for sleazy behavior.

item.title: Charlie Ergen, SpaceX and Trump
item.description: The private market for spectrum may rescue dumb industrial policy.

item.title: Trump’s Iran Target List, Good and Bad
item.description: Hitting desalination and power plants would hurt the Iranian people.

item.title: Iran’s Regime Says: Lebanon Is Ours
item.description: Tehran’s ambassador refuses to leave Beirut, as if he owns the place.

item.title: Why Not Declare War?
item.description: Congress hasn’t done it since 1942. But a case may emerge for doing it in Iran.

item.title: The Pols Who Failed Sheridan Gorman
item.description: A young woman’s murder has Chicago officials rushing to evade responsibility.

item.title: The World Needs a Stronger U.N.
item.description: Powerful nations are flouting international law, and somebody ought to stop them.

item.title: The Cost of a Constantly Online Childhood
item.description: Teens who use social media for three or more hours a day face increased risk of depression and anxiety.

item.title: Florida Empowers Public-Sector Employees
item.description: The Sunshine State sides with workers and holds unions accountable to deliver value.

item.title: The Abraham Accords Passed the Stress Test
item.description: Cooperation between the U.A.E. and Israel has strengthened during the Iran war, and there will be more partnership opportunities when the fighting ends.

item.title: Operation Epic Fury Should Make China Very Afraid
item.description: Like Desert Storm in 1991, a stunning air campaign is demonstrating America’s power of deterrence.

item.title: How Democrats Can Use Their Coming Majority
item.description: Investigations of Trump won’t satisfy the voters. To win in 2028, the party needs a positive agenda.

item.title: Kings of Dark Money
item.description: ‘No Kings’ propaganda is well-funded and Beijing couldn’t be happier.

item.title: New Options for Your 401(k)
item.description: The Labor Department aims to free retirement plans from regulatory overreach and litigation abuse.

item.title: Pepper…and Salt
Trump’s Gift to the IMF and China
item.description: A U.S. quota increase will do more for Beijing than for America.

item.title: More Right-Wingers Ban Trans Athletes
item.description: Actually, it was the International Olympic Committee, and sportscaster Bob Costas approves.

item.title: Democrats Revive a Jimmy Carter Tax Mistake on ‘Windfall’ Profits
item.description: They want to reduce the price incentive to boost oil and gas production.

item.title: Sayonara, Sora: OpenAI Says Fun Time Is Over
item.description: In killing the video-generation app, the company’s priority is no longer wowing the media.

item.title: Are We Headed for Federal Control of College Sports?
item.description: The Supreme Court took away the NCAA’s ability to regulate. Now Congress is thinking of intervening.

item.title: Side Quests Lead to Big Wins
item.description: Some of the most successful products have been discovered through surprises.

item.title: Trump, Powell and the Perils Ahead for the Fed
item.description: The president’s meddling with the central bank’s independence spells trouble in the future.

item.title: ‘I Will Support and Defend’
item.description: Young volunteers whose lives change with these words.

item.title: Medical Schools’ Rigor Is Not Under Threat
item.description: Stop eroding trust in the next generation of doctors.

item.title: Virginia Binges on Taxes, but Not Those Taxes
item.description: The real threats are a new payroll tax, a carbon tax on electricity producers and two pending proposals.

item.title: Allies, Obligations and the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Readers discuss whether America’s allies have a duty to help in the war against Iran.

item.title: What Do Democrats Mean by ‘Democracy’?
item.description: They claim Trump is an authoritarian, but their agenda seems to be ensuring one-party rule.

item.title: The Wages of ‘Land Acknowledgments’
item.description: Courts and other government actions threaten property rights in Canada.

item.title: Pepper…and Salt
Anthropic and Hegseth Need a Truce
item.description: The AI firm has a strong case against its ban as a ‘supply-chain risk.’

item.title: The High-Tax Wealth Flight Continues
item.description: IRS data show that taxpayers keep fleeing rapacious states even after the pandemic.

item.title: Da Bears Want Da Subsidies
item.description: Taxpayers could get sacked, as two states vie for a football arena.

item.title: AI Doesn’t Have to Rot Your Mind
item.description: If you use your chatbot as a coach, not a crutch, it can help you hone your own memory and reasoning skills.

item.title: California’s Golden Goose Is Already Flying the Coop
item.description: The state’s tax base is being hollowed out even before the ‘wealth tax’ qualifies for the ballot.

item.title: Trump’s Iran Strategy Is Chaotic
item.description: Perhaps this would be a good time to remember that the real endgame lies with the Iranian people.

item.title: How to Pass the SAVE America Act
item.description: Americans want winning policies, not performative politics, infighting or government shutdowns.

item.title: Far Left vs. Farther Left
item.description: The Minneapolis City Council may consider addressing the problems of Minneapolis.

item.title: Chicagoans Reject Brandon Johnson’s Agenda
item.description: Our polling has found that residents of the Windy City consistently disagree with the mayor’s priorities.

item.title: History Can Teach Us About ‘Settled Science’
item.description: Knowledge is always evolving, and we could do with a little more scientific humility.

item.title: The Evergreen State’s Addiction to Spending
item.description: Rather than raising taxes, Washington lawmakers need to get serious about sound budgeting.

item.title: Cuban Communism’s Long Goodbye
item.description: Everywhere else, the Cold War ended 35 years ago. Fidel Castro’s legacy may be crumbling at long last.

item.title: The Astroturf Podcasts
item.description: Tucker Carlson wants you to believe he speaks for MAGA. So do the Democrats.

item.title: Can the Federal Reserve Please Shut Up?
item.description: The central bank’s faulty economic predictions hurt markets and the Fed’s own institutional credibility.

item.title: ‘What $136 Trillion Looks Like in Your Living Room’
item.description: Is a constitutional amendment the only answer to Washington’s overspending?

item.title: Muslims Win if Iran’s Regime Loses
item.description: A defeat for the mullahs would yield benefits that would ripple across the globe.

item.title: Starbucks to Nashville?
item.description: The coffee giant signs a big lease in Tennessee.

item.title: AI Titans Work Hard to Discourage Working
item.description: New studies demonstrate what should be obvious: Universal basic income programs kill initiative.
